lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Arr? Be ye wantin' te go on account with our gang o' fillibusters?

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: The powder monkey be takin' a caulk after gettin' rowdy on bumboo, so there be plenty of room for ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiiiight...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll just be over here if you need me.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Yer standin' in me light stranger. Can I sell ye somethin' that'll make ye move?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Probably, let's see what you have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I doubt it.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Then move on stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That depends on if you have any cutlasses.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Cutlasses! Think yer a pirate now [lad/lass/kid]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, yes.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well, I have some cutlasses.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: I have Brass Hand Harry's Cutlass. Well, a load of them to be honest.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Then there's Unlucky Jenkins' Lucky Cutlass. Lovely blade that.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: I've also got Gentleman Mallard's Rapier. Not a cutlass I know, but the finest blade ever wielded by a privateer.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Now, which would ye like to hear about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brass Hand Harry's Cutlass.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: A good choice!

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Ye may end up meeting Brass Hand Harry if ye hang around long enough.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: He lost his left arm to a canister round, but that didn't stop him much.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Then he lost his right hand at the wrist fightin' the excise men off the coast of Karamja.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Not bein' the sort to lie about mopin', he asked me to make him a cutlass with a brass gauntlet fixed to the handle, and rig it up with a load of straps so he could keep fightin'.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: He's a great man is Harry, but he keeps snappin' the straps while he's at sea, so he sells the cutlasses back to me in exchange for a new one.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: It's quite a good blade, but the big, brass gauntlet is what makes it worthwhile. It's big enough to fit over even a real gauntlet, so ye get extra protection to yer sword hand.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: I can sell ye one fer 1040 coins.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Interested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Smart [lad/lass/kid]! Come and see me when ye have the coins fer it.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Here ye go. I've got a load of these left over if ye need more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well I'll still be here if ye change yer mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unlucky Jenkins' Lucky Cutlass.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Arr, tis a sad tale to this sword.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Jenkins was the lookout on the Jonah, a cursed vessel if there ever was one.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: One day his ship were attacked by another group of pirates durin' a fierce storm. One of their bowmen took a shot at the Cap'n of the vessel, but it were deflected off his compass and hit Jenkins in the back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How unlucky!

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: That weren't the worst of it [lad/lass/kid]. He survived the fight, and the ship's surgeon was trying to get the arrow out when...

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Are ye familiar with the spell bones to bananas?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, why?

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well, due to a blow to the head he took in the fight, the surgeon cast that spell, thinking it was some sort of new healing spell.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: And he did so while touching Jenkins' ribs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that killed him?

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: No [lad/lass/kid]!

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: See, he survived, his bones turned to bananas, as a huge storm blew up and the crew debated what to do with him.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: But you see, the storm was actually the tail end of a hurricane that had swept the nearby jungle islands, and when it approached the ship it petered out, droppin' all manner of strange things onto the deck and into the

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: ocean.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: The worst part was when the ship was bombarded with ravenous, hunger-crazed monkeys, the majority of which landed on Jenkins as he lay recoverin'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that killed him?

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: No [lad/lass/kid]!

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: See, what happened next, after the monkeys had had their way with him was...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I don't want to hear any more. Why would I want to buy his cutlass?

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well, after the poor man passed on...

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: In a final, terrifying, mashed potato coated tragedy...

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: His shipmates found his cutlass was completely untouched.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: See, having been exposed to all the bad luck that Jenkins went through, the thing is unlikely to ever see anything that bad again.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Hence, it must be lucky.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Ye can have it fer 2560 coins.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Interested?

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Smart [lad/lass/kid]! Come and see me when ye have the coins fer it.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Here ye go. I hope it brings you luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well I'll still be here if ye change yer mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gentleman Mallard's Rapier.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well, I'll tell ye the story, but it'll take a great deal of convincin' for me to sell you this rapier.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: See, gentleman Mallard is a legend in these parts. His family had been rich, shipping all sorts of goods up and down the coast.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Then their rivals got together and had their ships sunk, one after the other, and his business partners were all either bought off or had...accidents.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: With no money and only one ship left, he took to the life of a pirate, but no brigand was he.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: He took only from those men that had brought about his downfall, was fair with his men, and generous with his loot.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Why, this island would have been wiped out by the shakes if he hadn't turned over his stash to buy medicine.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: One day his ship was attacked by a huge sea creature that swept the decks of men with its tentacles.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: The crew took to the lifeboats, and the last they saw of him the gentleman was stabbin' at the thing as it hauled the ship under the water.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: A week later about ten foot of tentacle washed up on the beach, with his rapier embedded in it to the hilt.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: I took it, cleaned it up, and I've been lookin' after it ever since.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: I can't let it go for less than 25600 coins, and even then that's a bargain.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Ye think ye have what it takes to wield it?

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Smart [lad/lass/kid]! Come and see me when ye have the coins fer it.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: I hope yer worthy enough to wield this, stranger. I'd hate te think Gentleman Mallard would be disappointed in me fer sellin' it to ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well I'll still be here if ye change yer mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this anchor, can you fix it?

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well, this is a very unusual job. This thing looks more suited to bashin' peoples heads in than securing a ship.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Where did ye get it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You wouldn't believe me if I told you.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Stole it eh? Well I can sort it out for 230000 coins, if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's too much for me at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Right, let's get to work.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: There ye go, good as new!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Smith!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Smith
lines.text: Well I'll still be here if ye change yer mind.
